title: Registering Type Libraries
text: Registering Type Libraries For COM clients to locate and load an object, you need to register the object’s class in the Microsoft Windows Registry. The type information for the class is not copied to the Registry, but there are entries in the Registry that point to the type library file within the COM Interface DLL. These same entries indicate how to start the class’ server. When the COM Interface DLL is copied to a runtime workstation, you need to register the type library explicitly. Use the standard Microsoft Windows regsvr32.exe program to register the required type library. For example: regsvr32 servs.dll Copy To unregister a type library, use the /u switch. For example: regsvr32 /u servs.dll Copy Create configuration definitions in the Registry using the Uniface COM Configuration utility. To start the utility, choose Uniface COM Configuration from the Windows Start menu, or run ucomcfg.exe . Click New and provide a name for the definition. Set the values for the configuration files and directories required by the application. For more information, see Settings for Selected Configuration Definition . Click OK or Apply to save the definition. Note: For deployment within large organizations, .reg (registry) files can be used, or a third-party installation tool. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
